doom.driver: Trump says he's postponing 'scheduled attack of Iran tomorrow' at Middle East leaders' request. The U.S. and Iran are in a stalemate centered on the Strait of Hormuz, the global oil pathway whose closure due to the war has caused an energy supply shock.
doom.summary: The phrase 'scheduled attack' doing a lot of heavy lifting in today's news cycle — turns out the US had a Tuesday Iran strike on the calendar like a dentist appointment, and Gulf state leaders politely asked to reschedule. Meanwhile Xi is hosting Putin four days after hosting Trump, which is either masterful diplomacy or the world's most dangerous hot potato tournament. Ebola continues its grim upward trajectory at 131 dead, Treasury yields are testing 1999 highs, and Japan is quietly dumping US bonds — it's a full-spectrum bad day dressed in negotiation language.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: A US strike on Iran was literally scheduled for Tuesday and only postponed at Gulf state request, while Xi hosts Putin days after Trump in a breathtaking display of great-power triangulation.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Trump treating an Iran military strike as a negotiating chip — postponable on request — signals a level of crisis improvisation that keeps allies and enemies equally off-balance.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Iran has started Bitcoin-backed ship insurance for Hormuz strait transits, which is either creative sanctions evasion or the most cyberpunk geopolitical development of 2026.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Anduril and Meta's smart glasses prototype enabling drone strikes via eye-tracking represents a meaningful leap in AI-enabled lethal autonomy, quietly buried under the Musk-Altman verdict headlines.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Musk losing his OpenAI suit resolves one governance distraction, but the underlying question of who should control frontier AI remains as unsettled as ever.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing — ground-level upward view from cracked asphalt, camera pressed nearly flat to the pavement of a gas station forecourt somewhere in coastal Oman or the UAE, looking steeply skyward past the underside of an empty canopy overhang, its fluorescent tube lights dead and dark, the price sign above blank and unlit, every pump nozzle hanging limp and idle in its cradle, a long queue of cars stretching back into the deep background, headlights burning amber in the dusk, exhaust fumes rising in crimson-tinged columns against a bruised purple and charcoal sky, the underside of the forecourt canopy dominating the upper frame in deep shadow with rusted seams and flickering electrical conduit, a single emergency generator light casting a harsh cone of red-orange on the nearest pump casting long pixel-stepped shadows across the tarmac, crumpled receipts and a crushed plastic bottle skittering in a dry wind beneath the canopy's edge, the horizon behind the car queue glowing a deep smoldering amber as if the entire Gulf is backlit by something not quite sunset, color palette locked to dark crimson, charcoal grey, and burnt amber throughout, oppressive high-contrast lighting with no softness, firelight quality to every surface.

dawn.driver: Scientists found a smarter Mediterranean diet that slashes diabetes risk by 31%. A large European study revealed that a lower-calorie Mediterranean diet paired with exercise and coaching dramatically reduced the risk of type 2 diabetes.
dawn.blurb: ONE DIET CHOICE CUTS DIABETES RISK BY A THIRD
dawn.summary: In a world where headlines tilt dark — Ebola emergencies, military standoffs, mosque shootings — a large European study quietly delivered a landmark result: pair a Mediterranean diet with modest exercise and coaching, and type 2 diabetes risk drops by 31%. That's not a supplement company's claim; that's a rigorous population-level finding that could reshape how hundreds of millions of people eat and live. When the tools to prevent one of the planet's most burdensome chronic diseases turn out to be olive oil, vegetables, and a walk — that's genuinely good news worth sitting with.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: A 31% reduction in diabetes risk through diet and lifestyle is among the most actionable public-health findings in years, reproducible without pharmaceuticals or expensive interventions.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: The study was a large-scale European collaboration, demonstrating that cross-border research partnerships continue to produce meaningful results even amid geopolitical friction.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: The San Diego mosque security guard Amin Abdullah died protecting his community from a shooter, a quiet act of selfless courage that cut through a brutal day.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Conservative Utah cities and towns are building renewable energy coalitions, proving clean energy transitions can happen across political divides when local economics align.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Researchers discovered a hidden 'sugar code' on human cell surfaces using Glycan Atlasing — a potential early-detection breakthrough for cancer and other diseases hiding in plain sight.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Interior scene, we are inside a modest Spanish kitchen at mid-morning looking outward through a wide open doorway, warm golden Mediterranean light flooding in from a sun-drenched terracotta courtyard beyond, rich emerald herb pots framing the doorframe. The aftermath of a shared meal: a wooden kitchen table in the foreground holds the remnants of a Mediterranean spread — a near-empty glass bottle of olive oil catching amber light, a small terracotta bowl with a few remaining olives, scattered walnut shells, a halved lemon, torn whole-grain bread crust. A soft tape measure lies loosely coiled beside a handwritten food journal left open, small checkmarks visible on the page. A pair of well-worn colorful walking shoes sit neatly by the doorstep, angled toward the sunlit courtyard outside where the blurred suggestion of two older figures walk away together down a dappled stone path into warm emerald garden shade. The kitchen walls are warm ochre plaster, a hanging bunch of dried herbs casting a long shadow. Light is saturated golden afternoon honey, deep emerald shadows, the whole scene quiet and settled — not the meal itself but its peaceful, satisfied morning-after.
